description: Settlement governance loop: ADOPT THE REDESIGN PATH (fork resolved by Jordan — ratify/stage governance_play_redesign_v1, not revise-from-scratch). settlement_layer_v30 §3.2 offers one mandatory verb from four stat-pumps/season; governance_play_redesign_v1.md self-diagnoses the collapse ('roll one die a season') and its G1 prerequisite (sim/territory/registry.py + ledger, 6 passing tests) is already built. ACTION: stage the redesign toward ratified canon (per-stage gates fine); until the first stage lands, add the proposal to CURRENT.md's settlement row and HANDOFF_SE pending items so it cannot orphan. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction on PR #77; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-04-ners-qualitative-audit/ed_options.md E-1 (audit F-1)).

Accord/Order stacking ruling (edge-playability EP-9). scale_transitions_v30 §5.5 says personal Accord Domain Echoes 'do not stack with faction-level Govern actions in the same territory the same season — whichever produces the higher Accord applies'; peninsular_strain_v30 §2.7 (the CURRENT.md-listed head) says personal-scale changes and governor actions 'stack normally... cap +/-1 per source per settlement per season'. Neither cites the other; Accounting §7 Step 4c resolves neither. The player-and-governor-improve-the-same-settlement loop has an undecidable outcome. ACTION: Jordan rules which text wins; the loser is corrected with a supersession marker and Step 4c gains the resolving clause. Cheapest P1 in the audit. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 ('Ratify all' on PR #81; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-edge-playability-audit/edge_playability_audit_v1.md §7 item 4.

OPT-9, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). Armature keying wave (executes ED-IN-0014 with the C-MBSE B4 census): the armature §3 candidate types + gate-differentiated emit clauses (g_ord0/g_def0/g_dv0/siege/L-PS-drift/CI-milestones/era gates incl. the gateless IP=100). The 80-vs-75 CI milestone discrepancy routes to armature §5.11's ruling first (ACCEPT data-driven default), not re-litigated here.

OPT-16, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). Execute ED-SE-0001's own ordered updates (CURRENT.md settlement row + HANDOFF_SE pending item) -- the F-1 orphaning recurred live after the remediation that was supposed to prevent it (C-NERSPESS-4). RESOLVED 2026-07-07 (Stratum-A execution, this PR): executed ED-SE-0001's ordered updates — CURRENT.md settlement row + a standalone HANDOFF_SE pending item now track governance_play_redesign_v1.

RATIFIED subtractive verdicts for the SE action set (ED-IN-0027; decision ratified, EXECUTION this SE-lane follow-up; coordinate with ED-SE-0001 governance_play redesign / OPT-16 staging). Trade PRUNE (a bare income roll with zero settlement/faction/NPC effect unlike every sibling — closes the module_contracts settlement_economy retirement recommendation); Grant/Revoke Subnational Mgmt PRUNE + re-home to FA (it is a Provincial-Authority Domain Action, not SE-native — single-ownership via C-FA-12/domain_actions); Sponsor/Sponsor-event MERGE (resolves an as-specified dominant-strategy: §9's no-downside flat version strictly dominates the Debt-bearing redesign version); Investigate DISTILL (share the check math with the event-deck engine, redesign §5.2 build item 3 — not the FI scene-graph apparatus); Administer DISTILL (risk-free info half dominates Investigate; maintenance half subsumed by Keep Order Consent); Fund Development MERGE (content-free die-bonus -> folds into Develop); Treat REFINE (spec the chit call-in trigger for the no-GM engine). Docket rows: ed_options.md SE section. · EXECUTED 2026-07-08 (SE-lane follow-up to ED-IN-0027, resolves this ED). player_agency_v30 §9: Trade PRUNED (folded into Guild-contracts income), Fund-settlement-development MERGED into Develop funding, free Sponsor-settlement-event retired into the Debt-bearing Sponsor. settlement_layer_v30: §3.2 Administer annotated DISTILL (info->Investigate, maintenance->AP-not-spent; physical fold at OPT-16), §3.3 Grant/Revoke annotated as an FA-owned da.public_governance Domain Action (single-home ED-FA-0002). governance_play_redesign_v1 (proposal): Investigate reuses the fieldwork Investigation resolver, Treat chit call-in trigger specified (Debt tag via Friction card, §1.6/§2.3), Sponsor/Administer folds noted. module_contracts settlement_economy: SE-side retirement confirmed. combat_v30 stale Trade pointer fixed. No sim code (SE has no player-verb execution code). Gates green.

SE-1 (historical-precedent docket, HIGHEST PRIORITY per the report's own sequencing -- 'unblocks everything'): Weberian L/PS derivation-event table, converting registry.py's inert legitimacy/popular_support fields into a specified pipeline. L-channel (traditional/legal-rational): entry oath/privileges confirmed (+1 one-time), charter granted (+1), regular court held (+1 cap), stance-honoring tax collection (0; broken -> -1). PS-channel (charismatic/performance): dearth relieved (+2), festival sponsored (+1), settlement defended (+2), levy taken (-1), forced billeting (-1), public execution of a local (-2). Names the existing SS1.8 Mandate-drift feedback as 'routinization of charisma.' Grounding: Weber 1922 (traditional/legal-rational/charismatic typology maps directly onto the L/PS split); Hirschman 1970 (loyalty as damping). Target: new settlement_layer_v30 SS1.8 subsection. Magnitudes sim-calibrated before ledgering.

SE-2: Dearth chain + granary, replacing settlement_layer SS4.3's one-line famine row. Dearth triggers on entitlement failure (Prosperity 0, grain-route cut per SE-3, or Extraction stance in a Prosperity<=1 settlement), not just weather. Governor response verbs: Open Granary (granary>=1, PS+1 -- Scott 1976 remission), Fix Prices (Order 0, Guild Disposition -1, black-market check -- Thompson 1971 taxation populaire pre-empted), Requisition (displaces dearth to a neighbor), Ignore (Order-2, PS-2, riot targets millers/merchants first per Thompson's documented targeting order). New verb Provision (granary+1, W cost). Grounding: Thompson 1971; Scott 1976; Sen 1981 (entitlement-failure trigger, not availability); Rome's cura annonae; Will & Wong 1991 (Ming-Qing changpingcang granaries). Rides the ED-SE-0001 governance_play_redesign ratification track. AUTHORED (2026-07-08): drafted into settlement_layer_v30.md new §4.3a (Dearth chain + granary) as PROPOSED, per the SE-1 (§1.8a) Weberian L/PS pipeline this entry depends on. No sim code (SE has no player-verb execution code yet, per the ED-SE-0005 precedent) -- ratification tracks the ED-SE-0001 governance_play_redesign staging.

SE-3: grain routes -- mechanical teeth for the open geography_v30 item ED-054/BALANCE-005 ('Hafenmark food dependency has no mechanical teeth'). Tag breadbasket provinces (Feldmark, Stillhelm) as grain sources; non-breadbasket settlements need a traced adjacency route to a source or Port; a cut route (occupation/blockade/siege of a waypoint) puts dependents one season from Dearth (SE-2). Makes Hafenmark's dependency the mechanic, not just an unimplemented complaint. Grounding: Athens' Black Sea grain law; Rome's Egyptian grain fleet; Hanseatic Baltic grain via Danzig; Sen 1981 (siege as entitlement warfare). Route-tracing granularity (per-province vs per-settlement) flagged inline, defaulting to per-province -- reuses only existing adjacency-graph machinery. AUTHORED (2026-07-08): drafted into settlement_layer_v30.md new §4.3b (Grain routes) as PROPOSED, closing the open geography_v30 ED-054/BALANCE-005 item mechanically (breadbasket-tagged provinces, route-tracing defaulting to per-province granularity, reusing only the existing adjacency graph). No sim code yet.

SE-4: re-ground settlement_layer SS3.3's grant/revoke pair with charter/prescription/quo warranto mechanics. Grant writes a durable Charter tag (survives succession); revocation Ob rises with charter age (prescription -- privilege hardens into right); revoking a charter older than ~16 seasons is a public Quo Warranto social-contest scene, and on success still costs Order-1 peninsula-wide in every settlement that faction-type manages (London 1683's chilling effect). Confirmation-for-fee at transfer/succession: W+1 + settlement L+1 for confirming existing charters. Grounding: Magdeburg-law charter diffusion; Edward I's 1278-94 quo warranto campaign; City of London 1683/1690; universal accession-confirmation practice. Refines an existing PROVISIONAL rule in place. AUTHORED (2026-07-08): drafted into settlement_layer_v30.md new §3.3a (Charters/prescription/Quo Warranto) as PROPOSED, refining the existing §3.3 Grant/Revoke pair in place. No sim code yet.

SE-5: Entry Terms (Confirm vs Impose) at every settlement control transfer (conquest, treaty cession, seizure) -- the currently-missing transfer-boundary L/PS seeding rule LPS-2e never specified (it only seeds L/PS at game start, faction_state_authoring SS8). Confirm Privileges (Joyeuse Entree model): keep Charter/Precedent tags, L seeds 3, fiscal stance capped at Standard for 4 seasons. Impose Administration: strip tags, L seeds 1, no cap, Order-1. Composes with (does not replace) faction_layer SS2.3's existing Accord-on-transfer table. Grounding: Joyeuse Entree of Brabant 1356; Henry I's 1100 coronation charter; Olson 1993 (Impose = roving-bandit pricing). Target: new settlement_layer SS5.3. AUTHORED (2026-07-08): drafted into settlement_layer_v30.md new §5.3 (Entry Terms at control transfer: Confirm Privileges seeds L=3 / Impose Administration seeds L=1) as PROPOSED. Partially LIVE in sim: sim/provincial/faction_action.py's ED-FA-0013 conquest-terms fork reads this §5.3 seed value directly (Territory.entry_terms_l_seed = 3 on a Terms outcome) -- a dormant, non-serialized proxy pending the LPS-1 relocation of Legitimacy onto Settlement, but the two lanes' numbers are reconciled (not independently drifting).

SE-6: succession-continuity patch for faction_behavior SS3.5.1 / settlement_layer SS1.8 -- 'normal' succession leaves settlement L unchanged (the corporate crown never dies, so no interregnum-exposure penalty applies); the SS1.8 +1 confirmation bonus applies only where an entry/confirmation ceremony is actually performed (ties to SE-5). Contested/emergency/imposed succession modes: L-1 in all held settlements + Regency check (FA-7). Grounding: Kantorowicz 1957, The King's Two Bodies -- the fiction was engineered specifically to abolish the interregnum. AUTHORED (2026-07-08): drafted into settlement_layer_v30.md new §1.8b (Succession continuity) as PROPOSED -- corrected during drafting to reference the canonical `state.succession` Key / `succession_mode` field rather than a non-canonical dotted-path name. No sim code yet.

SE-7 (mixed -- residencia/visita/rotation core promote-ready, the venal-appointment variant is a FORK needing Jordan on tone: does Valoria sell offices?): names and completes governance_play_redesign SS1.4's oversight toolkit. Visita (surprise inspection: reveals true settlement state, adjusts suspicion), Residencia (mandatory end-of-tenure audit scene -- a social contest where accumulated Grudge/Debt tags ARE the evidence dossier), Rotation policy (governors rotate every N seasons: clears local Grudge/capture risk at the cost of Order-1 + forfeited Precedent bonuses -- the rule-of-avoidance's known price). Grounding: Castilian/Indies residencia & visita; Ming-Qing rule of avoidance + Censorate; Ottoman timar rotation (and its malikane decay as the venality warning the optional variant risks). Rides ED-SE-0001.

SE-8 (mixed -- Advowson promote-ready, Local Interdict a FORK needing Jordan -- adds a Church roster action, same class of call as other blocked Church-roster items): parish-level church-state seam. (a) Advowson: every settlement with a religious building gets a patron (default Church, grantable/purchasable/claimable as a Charter-class tag) who nominates its priest, biasing Persuasion-Track drift and church_attention; contested via a Hold-Court-style court scene (darrein presentment). (b) NEEDS JORDAN -- Local Interdict: Church action vs one settlement suspending Parish Social Services + Order/PS drain toward the controller, with a cumulative resentment check that can cost Church L in that settlement too (John's England, Venice 1606 -- a genuinely two-edged instrument). Grounding: advowson & the assize of darrein presentment; Constitutions of Clarendon 1164/Becket; Innocent III's 1208-14 interdict of England.

SE-9 (mixed -- (b) marcher-autonomy derivation promote-ready, (a) Plant-Colony action is a FORK needing Jordan since it touches the fixed 37-settlement registry and must be framed as CONVERTING an existing Village/Outpost, never adding a node): military colony + marcher autonomy, deriving registry.py's declared-but-unread governor_emergence field. (a) NEEDS JORDAN -- Plant Colony: frontier Village/Outpost -> garrison-colony (Defense+1 permanent, militia defends without upkeep, L seeds 5 toward the founder, PS seeds 1 -- natives displaced). (b) Marcher autonomy: frontier-province governors accrue suspicion at half rate but governor_emergence+1/season on defensive wins, feeding the existing GD-3 emergence pipeline at threshold. Grounding: Byzantine themata/stratiotika ktemata; Habsburg Militargrenze Grenzer bargain; Roman coloniae/limitanei; Welsh Marches palatine liberties.

SE-10: Weight loss as Exit (Hirschman closure) -- a settlement at >=2 consecutive seasons of Dearth or Order<=1 loses 1 Settlement Weight (emigration, lowering its Mandate contribution and Treasury base); recovers +1 per 4 stable seasons (capped as now). Exit destination gets a +0.5 Prosperity-growth season in the nearest higher-Order settlement. Closes the loop between existing 'population leaving' flavor text and the SS1.8 Weight aggregate. Grounding: Hirschman 1970 (Exit as the silent vote); Stadtluft macht frei nach Jahr und Tag (town freedom as the historical exit magnet). Target: settlement_layer SS1.8 (Weight) + SS4.3. AUTHORED (2026-07-08): drafted into settlement_layer_v30.md new §1.8c (Weight loss as Exit) as PROPOSED. No sim code yet.

Citation-patch CP-2 (core annotation promote-ready; an OPTIONAL founder-cohesion mechanic is a FORK needing Jordan, flagged separately, not blocking the annotation): annotate settlement_layer SS7.1's Generational Shift clock with Ibn Khaldun's Muqaddimah asabiyyah cycle (group cohesion strongest in the founding generation, decaying by the 3rd-4th as the dynasty urbanizes -- the classical theory of exactly what the clock hand-waves). OPTIONAL: a founder-cohesion Sta+1 that expires at Threshold 2 -- needs Jordan (a numeric/tone call, not implied by the annotation itself). AUTHORED (2026-07-08, core annotation only): drafted into settlement_layer_v30.md §7.1's Generational Shift clock as a CP-2 annotation grounding it in Ibn Khaldun's asabiyyah cycle (reworded 'cohesion' to 'solidarity' in-text to keep the deprecated-name lint clean of the unrelated Cohesion-stat rename). The OPTIONAL founder-solidarity Sta+1 mechanic remains unbuilt and needs_jordan, as this entry already flagged.

Bundle of two attribute/value coherence audit (ED-IN-0029) OPT-AV-18 sub-items, decision-ratified, execution deferred to the SE lane: (1) Fort Level province-to-settlement inheritance rule (census C5-F6) -- RATIFIED default: settlement value = province value (no separate settlement-level override absent an explicit exception). (2) Garrison / Local Economy / Public Order section-9 ratify-vs-[ASSUMPTION]-mark question (census C5-F2) -- the audit's critic steered this as SE-owned; RATIFIED to mark/ratify per SE's own review rather than leave as a bare [ASSUMPTION] tag. Decision-ratified, execution pending.

AUTHORED (2026-07-09): Kokudaka Survey -- Periodic assessment locks `assessed_base`; assessed/actual gap = surplus or neglect-revolt Grounding: Taikō Kenchi. Target: governance §1.3 new verb + §5.9. Landed as PROPOSED text at `governance_play_redesign_v1.md` new §1.3a. No sim code (SE/FA rank-ladder doc surfaces only).

AUTHORED (2026-07-09): Encabezamiento (Negotiated Quota) -- Fixed-term Compact (5th tag) locking a figure below live capacity + reciprocal Petition right Grounding: Cortes encabezamiento 1534. Target: governance §1.3 Levy method. Landed as PROPOSED text at `governance_play_redesign_v1.md` new §1.3a + §1.6 (5th Ledger tag family, Compact). No sim code (SE/FA rank-ladder doc surfaces only).

AUTHORED (2026-07-09): Goningumi cells -- Collective-liability 5-household cells; compounding Cell Revolt Grounding: Hideyoshi 1597 / gonin-gumi. Target: governance §1.3 Keep Order + §1.6 + §2.3. Landed as PROPOSED text at `governance_play_redesign_v1.md` new §1.3b. No sim code (SE/FA rank-ladder doc surfaces only).

AUTHORED (2026-07-09): Za patron lapse -- Privileges lapse automatically on patron's standing drop; new attack vector Grounding: Za guilds (Enryakuji/Kitano). Target: settlement §3.3a Charter + patron field. Landed as PROPOSED text at `settlement_layer_v30.md` new §3.3b. No sim code (SE/FA rank-ladder doc surfaces only).

AUTHORED (2026-07-09): Clerk Capacity -- Second AP source with hidden, Investigate-only Clerk Corruption (opaque liability) Grounding: Ming-Qing muyou/shuli. Target: governance §1.1a. Landed as PROPOSED text at `governance_play_redesign_v1.md` new §1.1a. No sim code (SE/FA rank-ladder doc surfaces only).

AUTHORED (2026-07-09): Ordenanza Ratification -- Ratify/Reject/Amend guild-authored rules; caste-exclusion lock-in Grounding: Spanish gremios. Target: governance §1.3 Hold Court branch. Landed as PROPOSED text at `governance_play_redesign_v1.md` new §1.3c. No sim code (SE/FA rank-ladder doc surfaces only).

AUTHORED (2026-07-09): Seggio Council -- Hereditary bodies w/ bespoke non-transferable privileges outside the Charter system; bloc-Treat Grounding: Naples Seggi. Target: settlement §3.3 + §4.5. Landed as PROPOSED text at `settlement_layer_v30.md` new §3.3c. No sim code (SE/FA rank-ladder doc surfaces only).

Gongsuo Registration -- Visibility/exposure toggle; invisible-safe vs visible-exploitable Grounding: gongsuo (late Ming-Qing). Target: governance §1.3a. Judged promote-ready (priority P2) but NOT authored into canon this session -- queued for a follow-on authoring pass per the docket's STEP 3 sequencing recommendation.

Convene the Circle -- Peer-settlement obligation pooling; first lateral governor-to-governor trust axis Grounding: Reichskreise 1512. Target: governance §1.3 + §1.6. Judged promote-ready (priority P2) but NOT authored into canon this session -- queued for a follow-on authoring pass per the docket's STEP 3 sequencing recommendation.

Borrow (financier loan) -- Loan vs named extractive right; financier-actor; reuses Quo Warranto clawback Grounding: Fugger / Habsburg mining. Target: governance §1.3 + §1.6 Concession tag. Judged promote-ready (priority P2) but NOT authored into canon this session -- queued for a follow-on authoring pass per the docket's STEP 3 sequencing recommendation.

Boschi Pubblici Requisition -- Directive tied to a named production dependency (supply chain); Defy starves the dependent building Grounding: Venetian forest law. Target: governance §1.4 Directive type. Judged promote-ready (priority P2) but NOT authored into canon this session -- queued for a follow-on authoring pass per the docket's STEP 3 sequencing recommendation.

Podestà (contracted outsider) -- Outsider governor + appointer-liability audit (malfeasance bills the principal faction's L) Grounding: podestà forestiero / sindacato. Target: settlement §3.2b. Judged promote-ready (priority P2) but NOT authored into canon this session -- queued for a follow-on authoring pass per the docket's STEP 3 sequencing recommendation.

Corregidor (overlay) -- Specialist layered *over* the sitting governor; self-expiring term-scoped tags Grounding: Corregidor 1480. Target: governance §1.4a. Judged promote-ready (priority P2) but NOT authored into canon this session -- queued for a follow-on authoring pass per the docket's STEP 3 sequencing recommendation.

Consolidated Command (Doux) -- Pooled-AP multi-settlement governor; Π=max, bloc-wide crisis Grounding: Byzantine doux/katepano. Target: governance new §1.9. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: Are multi-settlement governors (pooled AP, concentrated revolt risk, Weight-capped forced split) in scope for the governance layer? Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Legation Split -- Weight-threshold forces split of delegated multi-settlement authority Grounding: Paul III Romagna split 1540. Target: settlement §3.2a. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: Are multi-settlement governors (pooled AP, concentrated revolt risk, Weight-capped forced split) in scope for the governance layer? Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Guild Uprising (Compact/Suppress) -- One crisis, two durable outcomes; Concord = permanent guild veto on governor's Develop Grounding: Augsburg 1368 / Nuremberg 1348. Target: governance §2.3 + §1.6. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: May a governance outcome install a *permanent* guild veto on the governor's own Develop authority? Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Fiscal Tribunal -- Self-binding institution; NPC-initiated Petition can reverse a prior player action Grounding: Naples Sommaria 1444. Target: governance §1.3 new institution. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: May an NPC-initiated ruling reverse a prior player governance action without the player's consent? Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Chancellery Gatekeeper -- NPC can silently substitute a harsher Directive (information-asymmetry deception) Grounding: Silijian / pihong chokepoint. Target: governance §1.4a. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: May the no-GM engine silently, undetectably substitute a harsher Directive than the PA issued (deception of the player)? Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Status Freeze -- Caste-mobility lock trading Order for blocked advancement Grounding: shi-nō-kō-shō / katanagari 1588. Target: governance §1.3 (w/ Survey) + faction_politics Part 3. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: A caste-mobility lock that buys Order by hard-blocking advancement; tone call inside a caste system the corpus authors as critique. Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Salt Certificate -- Tradeable geography-siloed monopoly tokens + hereditary-franchise one-way door Grounding: Yanyin / gangfa 1617. Target: governance §1.3 Develop funding. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: A tradeable geography-siloed monopoly-token economy with a hereditary-franchise one-way door (CHN-4) and the batch's first calendar-driven decay clock (CHN-5). Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Kaizhongfa Colonize Directive -- First calendar-driven (not trigger) decay clock; timing pressure Grounding: Ming kaizhongfa 1370. Target: governance §1.4 Directive (frontier). NEEDS_JORDAN fork: A tradeable geography-siloed monopoly-token economy with a hereditary-franchise one-way door (CHN-4) and the batch's first calendar-driven decay clock (CHN-5). Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Reichsacht (outlawry) -- Status flag w/ contagion clause but enforcement-dependent teeth; a decree that can fail Grounding: Edict of Worms 1521. Target: governance §1.6 + §2.3 + faction_politics §1.0a. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: Should a Crown decree be visibly, legibly defiable and fail (contagion clause + enforcement-dependent teeth)? Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Mediatize -- Immediate/Mediatized status axis as treaty compensation currency Grounding: Reichsunmittelbarkeit / 1803. Target: governance §1.4 + settlement §2.1. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: May a settlement's direct-answer status be administratively demoted without conquest and traded as treaty compensation currency? Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Crush the Estates -- Irreversible SE-layer rewrite of Hafenmark's Alderman gate Grounding: Cortes of Tarazona 1592. Target: governance §1.6a + faction_politics §1.2. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: May an SE-layer action *irreversibly* rewrite a ratified faction's rank-ladder gate (Hafenmark's Alderman supermajority → crown appointment)? Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

State Arsenal -- Persistent Wage/Pension Debt (fires every season) + single-NPC-zeroes-output Grounding: Arsenale di Venezia. Target: governance §1.3 + settlement. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: A persistent per-settlement Wage/Pension Debt liability + a single-NPC-zeroes-output failure mode. Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Bonifiche Capital Posture -- Military loss triggers an economic choice; tag changes extraction *type* Grounding: Post-Cambrai bonifiche. Target: governance §1.6 + §2.3. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: A fifth Ledger tag family where a military *loss* triggers an economic choice that changes what *type* of extraction a Directive may ask for. *(Also the gate-question: are Ledger tag families meant to be extensible beyond the four?)* Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

Scuole Grandi -- Caste-excluded Civic Prestige as a *targeted* Π valve with a designed ceiling Grounding: Scuole Grandi (San Rocco). Target: governance §1.3 Sponsor + §2.1 Π. NEEDS_JORDAN fork: A caste-excluded Civic Prestige resource that modifies the Π homeostat (a balance-sensitive surface) as a targeted, ceilinged pressure valve. Not authored into canon this session -- queued.

settlement_layer_v30.md states the settlement-Prosperity -> faction-Treasury contribution with two incompatible multipliers for the SAME edge. SS1.3 derived-values table (L47): 'Prosperity | Local Economy | Prosperity x 50 | Gold income contribution to faction Treasury' -- the description column labels the x50 value itself as the Treasury contribution. SS1.8 (L169): 'faction Treasury income = Sigma settlement Prosperity x 10, derived_stats SS8.1'. Same quantity (a settlement's Prosperity rolled up into faction Treasury), x50 vs x10. Report SS3.1 resolved the instrument disagreement by direct read: this is a real internal conflict; module_adjudicator's 'two clean derivations' read is a syntactic artifact (its extractor node-typed 'Local Economy' and 'Treasury' as different targets and never compared their multipliers). Proposed remediation (Jordan): clarify whether Local Economy (x50) is a display value distinct from the x10 Treasury rollup, and correct SS1.3's description column either way. Note: L737 carries a separate mine-specific 'Province Treasury +50/season' flavor figure -- sweep in the same pass. Surfaced by the 2026-07-13 multi-agent audit (mechanic_audit GAP-01).
source: designs/audit/2026-07-13-multi-agent-audit/ (report SS3.1 / SS5 item 4 / mechanic_audit GAP-01); verified against settlement_layer_v30.md L47 vs L169

description: Compound settlement types 'Fortress-City' (S-014 Ehrenfeld, L358) and 'Cathedral-City' (S-036 Himmelenger, L429), plus 'Village' (14 of 37 registry rows), are used throughout PART 2's settlement registry but are ABSENT from SS1.2's Settlement Types table (L30-39: only Seat/City/Town/Fortress/Port/Cathedral/Mine/Outpost). 'Village' does appear in SS1.8's base(Type) Weight table (L160, Village=1), but the two COMPOUND types do not, leaving W_s (settlement Weight = base(Type)+Prosperity+FacilityTier, SS1.8) uncomputable for S-014 and S-036 -- which feed the size-weighted faction Mandate aggregate. SS3.2 Governor Eligibility (L541) is also keyed off settlement type. Proposed remediation: add Fortress-City/Cathedral-City (and Village) to SS1.2 and give the two compound types a base(Type) weight, or define them as decomposed districts. Surfaced by the 2026-07-13 multi-agent audit (mechanic_audit GAP-03).
source: designs/audit/2026-07-13-multi-agent-audit/ (report SS5 item 6 / mechanic_audit GAP-03); verified against settlement_layer_v30.md L30-39, L160, L358, L429

description: SS4.7 (Black Markets -- Niflhel Dissolution) modifies 'Settlement Wealth +0.5' (L877) and 'Settlement Accord -0.5' (L878). Neither is a valid settlement field under the SS1.3 REVISED schema, whose settlement stats are Prosperity/Defense/Order (0-5); 'Accord' is a PROVINCE-derived value (floor-average of settlement Order per SS1.3), not a settlement field, and 'Wealth' is not a settlement stat at all. SS4.7-SS4.9 read as un-migrated pre-settlement-layer legacy content still referencing the old settlement schema. Proposed remediation: migrate SS4.7-SS4.9's stat references onto Prosperity/Order (and drop 'Settlement Accord'), or mark the block superseded. Surfaced by the 2026-07-13 multi-agent audit (mechanic_audit GAP-04).
source: designs/audit/2026-07-13-multi-agent-audit/ (report SS5 item 7 / mechanic_audit GAP-04); verified against settlement_layer_v30.md L870-878 vs SS1.3 L53-61

description: Co-file drift: the prose companion settlement_adjacency_v30.md still documents the pre-PP-726 settlement graph while the data file designs/territory/valoria_geography_v30.yaml was rebuilt at PP-726. The prose SS1.2 header reads 'Canonical Adjacency Set (36 settlements)' (L34) and L36 cites PP-723's '49 edges' as canonical; the geography YAML (L569-587) explicitly notes 'SETTLEMENT-LEVEL ADJACENCY GRAPH (PP-726 supersedes PP-723)' with '55 edges across 37 march-target settlements' at corrected granularity ('PP-723's 49-edge block was at WRONG GRANULARITY'). The audit further flags S-ID reassignment drift across the co-files: e.g. settlement_layer_v30.md's PART 2 registry lists S-011 as 'Spelzdorf / Village' while both the geography YAML (L325) and the adjacency prose (L45/L54) treat S-011 as 'Stillhelm Watch' -- S-IDs are not consistent across settlement_layer_v30, settlement_adjacency_v30, and valoria_geography_v30.yaml. Implementing battles/movement/grain-dependency from the stale prose routes gameplay through the wrong settlements. Proposed remediation: rewrite the adjacency prose against the PP-726 geography YAML (37 settlements / 55 edges) and reconcile S-IDs across the three co-files. Surfaced by the 2026-07-13 multi-agent audit (mechanic_audit GAP-05).
